agent1_snippets.title: Oral Surgery, Instruments -- Introduction
agent1_snippets.content: Numerous instruments are utilized during oral surgical procedures, each having a specific role (see Image. An Oral Surgery Instruments Kit). Dental practitioners should be able to select appropriate instruments in given situations, preventing iatrogenic damage and traumatic procedures and thus increasing patient safety. This article introduces several commonly used instruments during various oral surgical procedures but does not provide an exhaustive list.

agent1_snippets.title: Oral Surgery, Extraction of Roots -- Equipment
agent1_snippets.content: Extraction of dental roots requires many of the same surgical instruments in conventional exodontia. The typical surgical setup usually includes a combination of the following: Elevators Luxators Forceps Root tip picks Retractors Curets Dental explorers Dental mirrors Scissors Scalpels Hemostats Periodontal Probes Rongeurs Bone files Local anesthetic needles and syringes Irrigation needles and syringes Local anesthetic Sterile gauze Sutures Surgical handpiece Surgical bur Sterile saline Sterile water Bite block

agent1_snippets.title: Colonoscopy -- Equipment
agent1_snippets.content: The colonoscope features a handle designed for left-hand operation, while the right hand manipulates and advances or withdraws the scope at the anal insertion point. Dials at the handle base permit scope rigidity adjustment, enabling navigation through challenging anatomical corners and applying variable tension during colonoscope advancement and withdrawal. The handle incorporates multiple manipulating devices, buttons for insufflation, suction, irrigation functions, and ports for instrument passage, including biopsy forceps, snares, clip appliers, and polypectomy devices. Additional working instruments attached to the handle facilitate image quality adjustment and videography control options during colonoscopic examination, including recording, freeze frame, image capture, and magnification functions.

agent1_snippets.title: Nasoseptal Flap -- Equipment
agent1_snippets.content: While a variety of endoscopes should be available, the flap is typically elevated with visualization provided by a 0 or 30-degree endoscope. An endoscopic monopolar electrocautery instrument with a needle-tip at a 45-degree bend provides ergonomic and precise incisions with simultaneous hemostasis. A Cottle elevator, micro-scissor, ball-tip probe, curette, curved olive-tip suction, through-cutting forceps (both straight and angled), and Blakesley forceps (both straight and angled) should be readily available and are typically found on a standard Functional Endoscopic Sinus Surgery (FESS) tray. These instruments allow for atraumatic handling and refining of the flap as it is raised.

agent1_snippets.content: Stapes surgery requires a complete set of middle ear instruments, including a sickle knife, House round knife, Bellucci scissors, Rosen pick, right-angle pick, Gimmick elevator, Alligator forceps, incudostapedial joint knife, fascia press, measuring probe, fine suction tips, and various sizes of otologic specula. Many surgeons favor the use of a speculum holder to facilitate the bimanual technique.

agent1_snippets.title: Oral Surgery, Instruments -- Nursing, Allied Health, and Interprofessional Team Monitoring
agent1_snippets.content: Four-handed dentistry can be utilized to maximize efficiency during oral surgical procedures. The dental nurse recognizes the instrument required next or is instructed by the dental practitioner and then provides this to them. [39] A sound understanding of oral surgical instruments is imperative as there are usually many instruments on the tray, and delays in treatment can result if the assistant cannot identify each instrument and pass it to the operator in good time. Additionally, the wider team must know various instruments to identify faulty pieces and report and replace them.

agent1_snippets.title: Oral Surgery, Instruments -- Clinical Significance
agent1_snippets.content: The instruments reviewed are fundamental to many oral surgical procedures. As such, it is necessary to be aware of their existence, appreciate the clinical situations they are used in, and understand how they can be applied. This knowledge facilitates their safe use, as improper instrumentation in combination with uncontrolled excessive force can have detrimental effects such as iatrogenic fracture of the mandible. [35]

agent1_snippets.title: Difficult Foley Catheterization -- Equipment -- Urethral Sounds and Dilators
agent1_snippets.content: Bougie-a-boules or "Bougies" : These instruments are slim metal rods with an olive-shaped, slightly tapered tip or head and a slightly flattened handle. They come in sizes from 8 to 40 French and are about 10" long. They are most often used for measurement or calibration of the urethral lumen as well as dilation. For active dilation, other instruments are usually preferred as the effective tip is quite short and the slim metal rod can bend.

agent1_snippets.content: If the gastrojejunostomy is to be performed laparoscopically, then the following equipment is required: Nasogastric or orogastric tube Liver retractor A laparoscope with a light source and monitor Insufflation equipment Laparoscopic trocars Bowel graspers Harmonic scalpel or Ligasure Electrocautery equipment Laparoscopic suction irrigator Laparoscopic linear cutting stapler or end-to-end anastomotic stapler depending on the planned technique Esophagogastroduodenal scope If the gastrojejunostomy is to be performed open, then the following equipment is required: Nasogastric or orogastric tube Liver retractor Bowel clamps Bowel forceps Harmonic scalpel or Ligasure Electrocautery equipment Suction irrigator Linear cutting stapler, end-to-end anastomotic stapler, or appropriate suture depending on the planned technique Needle driver Esophagogastroduodenal scope

agent1_snippets.title: Oral Surgery, Instruments -- Function -- Surgical Straight Handpiece
agent1_snippets.content: Most commonly, round burs are used to remove the alveolar bone. During tooth removal, surgeons may use a fissure bur for crown and root sectioning. [26] [27] Fissure burs are also used for coronectomy procedures, which involve removing the crown of the tooth and reducing the remaining roots 3mm below the crest of the buccal and lingual plates. [28] Bone rongeurs are instruments that are also used to aid bone removal. They can remove bony prominences or irregular ridges, which can be smoothed using a bone file, allowing for improved healing. [8]

agent1_snippets.content: Cleft palate repair sets should include the following: A fine suction cannula (e.g., Frazier 7 Fr) Mouth retractor (e.g., Dingman, Fisher, etc.) with tongue blades of different sizes. agent1_snippets.title: Ear Foreign Body Removal -- Equipment
agent1_snippets.content: Various methods exist for removing foreign bodies from the EAC. The type, shape, and location of the object, as well as the patient's level of cooperation, determine which equipment is most appropriate to use. Common instruments frequently employed include an otoscope with a removable lens, nasal speculum, alligator forceps, cup forceps, right-angle hooks, Schuknecht foreign body suction tips, curved Rosen picks, and balloon catheters, such as Fogarty catheters (see Image. Alligator Forceps). Binocular microscopy significantly enhances the use of precise and sharper instruments and is the preferred method for otolaryngologists.

agent1_snippets.title: Suprapubic Bladder Catheterization -- Equipment
agent1_snippets.content: Equipment utilized for the placement of a suprapubic catheter varies by technique. Typically, standard Foley catheters are used for drainage catheters. The open technique utilizes standard surgical instrumentation, including a small self-retaining retractor such as a Weitlander retractor and a dissolvable suture for closure of the cystostomy. The Percutaneous (Seldinger) technique requires a large-bore needle, a guide wire, and an away catheter insertion sheath. These are commercially available as an all-in-one kit. Percutaneous approaches are often performed under vision with a rigid or flexible cystoscope. Finally, a specialized retractor called a Lousley prostatic retractor can assist in an open tube placement. Portable ultrasound devices are also helpful in confirming tube location.

agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 2 -- Community 809
agent2_snippets.content: Vacuum dilatation of cervix is a specific medical operation used to facilitate delivery that belongs to the broader category of “Operation to facilitate delivery.” It is classified as a “procedure categorized by device involved,” reflecting that the technique relies on a vacuum device to dilate the cervix during childbirth. Because procedures categorized by device involvement are themselves a subset of “إجراء تدخلي” (interventional procedures), vacuum dilatation of cervix is an interventional procedure that utilizes specialized equipment to achieve its therapeutic goal. This hierarchical relationship highlights how the technique fits within both device‑based procedural classifications and the general domain of interventions aimed at assisting vaginal delivery.

agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2907
agent2_snippets.content: Application of therapeutic agent and internal obstetrical version are both classified as child procedures of surgical manipulation, indicating that each represents a specific type of operative intervention. As child entities under the broader category of surgical manipulation, these procedures involve direct manipulation of tissues or organs during a surgical setting; the application of therapeutic agent entails delivering a medication or biologic directly to a target site, while internal obstetrical version involves manually repositioning the fetus within the uterus. Recognizing both as subtypes of surgical manipulation helps organize procedural taxonomy, supports accurate clinical documentation, and guides appropriate patient care planning.

Within this framework, الولادة بالملقط (forceps delivery) is directly linked to فشل التوليد بالملقط, indicating that the failure represents a particular adverse event or subcategory of the forceps delivery procedure. Consequently, both الولادة بالملقط and its associated failure, فشل التوليد بالملقط, are situated under the larger grouping of other procedures that induce or assist delivery, highlighting their interrelated roles in assisted vaginal birth and the importance of recognizing potential complications.

agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 2 -- Community 1102
agent2_snippets.content: The collection of gynecologic interventions is organized under the overarching category “عملية للأعضاء التناسلية الأنثوية” (procedures for female genital organs). Within this category, major surgical procedures include “إزالة الرحم” (hysterectomy) and “استئصال المبيض” (ovarian removal), as well as “توسيع الرحم وكشطه” (uterine dilation and curettage). “توسيع الرحم وكشطه” further comprises specific techniques such as “Dilatation and curettage (D&C); aspiration after delivery or abortion” and its child procedure “شفط بالتخلية” (suction curettage). The procedure “Aspiration curettage following delivery or abortion” is classified as “Dilatation and curettage (D&C); aspiration after delivery or abortion,” indicating that it is a particular form of D&C performed after delivery or abortion. This hierarchical organization clarifies how various gynecologic surgeries are related, supporting clinicians in selecting appropriate interventions and understanding their place within comprehensive female reproductive care.

agent2_snippets.content: Surgical Manipulation serves as an overarching category that includes several specific obstetric procedures, namely Application of therapeutic agent, Combined obstetrical version, Internal obstetrical version, and Manual conversion of position. Within this hierarchy, Combined obstetrical version encompasses a more narrowly defined procedure called Internal and combined version without extraction, and this in turn includes Internal obstetrical version as a further specialized form. Both Internal and combined version without extraction and Manual conversion of position are explicitly linked to the uterus as their procedure site, indicating that these manipulations are performed on uterine tissue. This structured classification clarifies how various therapeutic and obstetric interventions are related to one another and highlights the uterine focus of the internal and manual conversion techniques, supporting clinicians in understanding procedural relationships and selecting appropriate interventions.

agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 3 -- Community 2516
agent2_snippets.content: Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery constitute a broad category of obstetric interventions that are employed to facilitate vaginal birth. Within this category, the specific complication فشل التوليد بالملقط (failure of forceps delivery) is identified as a child procedure, indicating that it is a particular adverse outcome associated with assisted delivery techniques. Episiotomy, a surgical incision made in the perineum to enlarge the vaginal opening, functions as a parent procedure within the same group, linking various delivery‑assisting methods and serving as a foundational technique for related interventions. Preventive incision, exemplified by episiotomy, is recognized as having a direct relationship to these obstetric procedures, underscoring the role of episiotomy as a preventive measure designed to reduce trauma during delivery and to support the successful execution of other assisted delivery methods. Together, these concepts illustrate how episiotomy and preventive incisions are integral components of the spectrum of procedures used to assist delivery, while also highlighting specific complications such as فشل التوليد بالملقط that can arise from assisted vaginal birth.

These actions are positioned within “OBSTETRICS: SURGICAL REPAIRS, CLOSURES AND RECONSTRUCTIONS” and connected to fetal monitoring through hierarchical links. Post‑partum obstetric procedures include manual removal of the placenta (بضع الارتفاق), evacuation of retained products of conception (تفريغ منتجات الحمل المحتجزة), dilation of the cervix uteri, and vacuum aspiration. Post‑partum obstetric operation is linked to “Zorg en onderzoek onmiddellijk na bevalling” (care and investigation immediately after delivery) and to repair of episiotomy. Across the taxonomy, “Procedure by site” and “Procedure on organ” are related by inverse‑ISA relationships, with “Procedure on organ” also serving as an inverse‑ISA of fetal monitoring, thereby integrating organ‑based procedures within the fetal monitoring framework. agent2_snippets.content: In the context of obstetric therapeutic procedures, “الإجراءات العلاجية الخاصة بالتوليد” represents the overarching category of interventions used in obstetrics. Within this category, “شفط بالتخلية” (suction curettage) functions as a primary or parent procedure that encompasses various techniques for uterine evacuation. One specific technique classified under suction curettage is “توسيع الرحم وكشطه” (uterine dilation and curettage), which is considered a child procedure of suction curettage. This hierarchical organization highlights that both suction curettage and dilation‑and‑curettage are integral components of the broader set of obstetric therapeutic interventions, guiding clinicians in the selection and application of appropriate uterine evacuation methods.

agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2071
agent2_snippets.content: Pregnancy-Related Procedures comprise a range of interventions used to manage complex obstetric conditions and facilitate safe delivery. Among these, the surgical treatment of ectopic pregnancy and abdominal pregnancy represents critical operative approaches for addressing abnormal implantation of the embryo, thereby preventing life‑threatening complications for the mother. In addition, Vaginal Assisted Delivery is included as a procedural option that supports the progression of labor and assists in the birth of the infant when additional support is required. Together, these child procedures under the umbrella of Pregnancy-Related Procedures illustrate the spectrum of surgical and assisted techniques employed in modern obstetric care.
